tickerText: [TENNIS] Roland-Garros: Mirra Andreeva (19) wins her 1st Grand Slam title, defeating Maja Chwalińska 6-3, 6-2! Men's Final: Alexander Zverev leading Flavio Cobolli 2-1 (6-1, 4-6, 6-4) • [BADMINTON] Indonesia Open: Victor Lai (CAN) def. Jonatan Christie (INA) 21-19, 21-8; An Se-young (KOR) def. Akane Yamaguchi (JPN) 23-21, 21-12 • [SQUASH] British Open: 18-year-old Amina Orfi becomes the youngest champion in history & Paul Coll clinches his 3rd crown in Birmingham!

newsItems.title: Mirra Andreeva Makes History at Roland-Garros
newsItems.excerpt: The 19-year-old phenom Mirra Andreeva captured her first Grand Slam singles title with a commanding 6-3, 6-2 victory over Maja Chwalińska, becoming the youngest champion in Paris since Monica Seles in 1992.

newsItems.title: Victor Lai and An Se-young Triumph at Indonesia Open
newsItems.excerpt: Canada's Victor Lai stunned home favorite Jonatan Christie in straight sets, while top-seeded An Se-young of South Korea overcame Japan's Akane Yamaguchi to secure the prestigious 2026 Indonesia Open crowns.

newsItems.title: Coll and Orfi Crowned 2026 British Open Champions
newsItems.excerpt: Egypt's 18-year-old Amina Orfi made history as the youngest women's British Open champion ever, while New Zealand's Paul Coll secured his third title in a thrilling series of finals in Birmingham.
